title: Baccharoides schimperi
intro: Baccharoides schimperi, also known as Schimper's Baccharis, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to East Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Baccharoides schimperi include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and it is also used in the production of herbal remedies such as cough syrups and digestive tonics.
